quick_facts.label: Recruiting channel
quick_facts.value: DRDO identifies CEPTAM as the recruitment channel for its DRTC, Administrative and Allied Group B and Group C cadres; Store Assistant A is governed as a Group C post.

quick_facts.label: Internal promotion provision
quick_facts.value: The 2023 amendment to the Store Assistant A/B recruitment rules provides that 5% of Store Assistant A vacancies may be filled by non-selection promotion from eligible Level-1 Group C employees with three years' regular service and a recognized-board Class 12 pass; the rules also refer to qualifying in a departmental examination.

sections.heading: What the stores function covers
paragraphs: DRDO’s published job description for a Stores Officer—not a post-specific duty statement for Store Assistant A—defines the stores function as supervising stores, ledger and bill work; managing receipt, verification, documentation, stacking/binning, preservation and issue of stores; coordinating audit matters; and total inventory management. This is useful operational context for understanding a Store Assistant role, but it should not be read as an exhaustive or binding job description for every Store Assistant A posting.
bullets: Likely work is records- and process-oriented: receiving and documenting material, maintaining store records, supporting issue/distribution processes, and working within inventory-control and audit procedures.
Exact allocation of duties, security requirements, software, material categories and laboratory/establishment posting should be confirmed from the appointment or vacancy notice.

sections.heading: How to enter through CEPTAM
paragraphs: For direct recruitment, follow the Store Assistant A-specific CEPTAM advertisement when it is issued. DRDO states that CEPTAM recruits for its Administrative and Allied Group B and Group C cadres. The last Store Assistant A-specific official Admin & Allied advertisement located for this guide is CEPTAM-10 (2022); it is evidence of the previous route, not confirmation that a Store Assistant vacancy is currently open.
bullets: Previous official CEPTAM-10 context: Store Assistant A was offered in English-typing and Hindi-typing variants; the published essential education context was a Class 12 pass from a recognized board or equivalent.
Previous official CEPTAM-10 context: Tier I was a CBT, followed for Store Assistant A by a qualifying typing test for shortlisted candidates.

Internal route: the 2023 recruitment-rule amendment provides a limited promotion channel into Store Assistant A for qualifying Level-1 Group C employees with the stipulated service and Class 12 qualification.

Do not assume that a Certificate in Computer Applications or a stores/inventory certificate is mandatory: the cited CEPTAM-10 requirement was Class 12 plus the prescribed typing test. Such certificates may still be useful preparation, but a future notification controls eligibility.

sections.heading: Skills to prepare
paragraphs: Typing is the clearest role-specific skill evidenced by the official CEPTAM-10 process: passing the typing test was essential for Store Assistant A. The stores-function description published by DRDO also supports preparation in accurate documentation, ledger/record work, verification, inventory handling and audit-conscious processes.
bullets: Computer typing in the language option specified in the notification.
Accuracy in data entry, document handling and maintaining records.
Basic inventory and material-handling concepts: receipt, verification, storage/binning, preservation and issue.
Careful adherence to procedures, because stores work interfaces with bills, vouchers, ledgers and audit.

sections.heading: Progression and departmental route
paragraphs: The official 2023 amendment confirms a departmental-examination-linked promotion provision into Store Assistant A: 5% of vacancies may be filled through non-selection promotion from qualifying Level-1 Group C employees who have completed three years of regular service and hold a Class 12 pass. Employees who qualify in excess of vacancies may be considered for later vacancies before employees qualifying at a later examination, as provided in the rule.
bullets: This rule supports a documented internal entry/progression route into Store Assistant A.

Although the recruitment rules cover Store Assistant A and Store Assistant B, the amendment cited here does not, by itself, establish the eligibility, timing or mechanism for promotion from Store Assistant A to Store Assistant B. Verify the then-current recruitment rules and departmental orders before treating Store Assistant B as an assured next step.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: In CEPTAM-10/Admin & Allied recruitment (2022), Store Assistant A was listed at 7th CPC Pay Level 2: ₹19,900–₹63,200 in the pay matrix, with other benefits and allowances under then-applicable Government of India rules. Basic pay, allowances, deductions and take-home compensation can vary with the appointment date, posting location, pay cell, government orders and future recruitment terms.
bullets: Use the pay level and matrix range as historical official context rather than a current salary quote.
Before applying, check the Store Assistant-specific official advertisement and appointment terms for the applicable pay level, allowances, age limits, vacancies and test standards.

useful_links.label: Store Assistant A/B Recruitment Rules amendment, 2023
